guide.titleHTML:
Whiskey & Walking-Horse Country
A Crossroads Road Trip
guide.sub: Four decades of back-road stories along one easy loop — from Lynchburg's whiskey hollow through Tullahoma's hometown square to the walking-horse country around Shelbyville.
guide.metaNote: every one a real Crossroads segment
guide.mapLede: Every stop along the route, plotted town by town.
guide.quote: “Come travel the highways and by-ways of Tennessee.”
guide.note: We hope this guide leads you to new memories, a hidden gem, or someplace you’ve always meant to visit. Thanks for making Tennessee Crossroads part of your journey.
guide.colophonHTML: Tennessee Crossroads is a Nashville PBS production · Each place began as a story on the show

print.coverCaption: Cascade Hollow, home of George Dickel — Tullahoma

sections.title: Lynchburg
sections.intro: The hollow that whiskey built — a courthouse square of smokehouses, candy kitchens, and cave-spring history.

stops.name: Jack Daniel's Distillery
stops.blurb: From the 1988 archives: the late Roger Brashears, Jack Daniel's longtime ambassador, walks us through the cave spring, the charcoal mellowing, and the hollow where it all began.

stops.name: Miss Mary Bobo's Boarding House
stops.blurb: Joe Elmore visits the Lynchburg landmark where dinner is served boarding-house style — heaping platters passed around shared tables, with a hostess to keep the stories coming.

stops.name: Barrel House BBQ
stops.blurb: Just off the Lynchburg square, Barrel House BBQ smokes its meat low and slow in a shop full of whiskey-town character. Joe Elmore follows his nose inside.

stops.name: Lynchburg Cake and Candy Company
stops.blurb: Tressa Bush stops where Lynchburg's signature whiskey cakes are baked from a family recipe — dense, buttery, and finished with a splash of the town's most famous export.

stops.name: Promise Manor
stops.blurb: A young family re-purposed this antebellum mansion as a wedding and event venue, and Cindy Carter finds the old home busy celebrating new beginnings.

sections.title: Tullahoma
sections.intro: A railroad town with vintage airplanes, gourmet surprises, and whisky spelled the Scotch way in the hollow next door.

stops.name: Beechcraft Heritage Museum
stops.blurb: Rob Wilds tours what may be the country's only museum devoted to a single make of airplane — hangars of gleaming Beechcraft history that draw pilgrims from across the map.

stops.name: George Dickel Distillery
stops.blurb: In Cascade Hollow north of town, George Dickel has made Tennessee whisky — spelled the Scotch way — since 1870. Rob Wilds follows the craft from spring water to barrel.

stops.name: Emil's Bistro & Marketplace
stops.blurb: A Franco-American romance turned a former mayor's home into some unexpectedly gourmet dining. Joe Elmore meets the husband-and-wife team behind Emil's.

sections.title: Shelbyville
sections.intro: The walking-horse capital — pencil makers, farm stays, and first-Saturday suppers in Bedford County.

stops.name: Musgrave Pencils
stops.blurb: A century on, Musgrave still turns Tennessee cedar into good old wood-case pencils. Joe Elmore visits the Shelbyville factory that has outlasted every trend in technology.

stops.name: Clearview Horse Farm
stops.blurb: In the walking-horse capital of the world, this farm bed-and-breakfast is hospitable to humans and horses alike — bring your own mount or just enjoy the pasture views.

stops.name: Flat Creek Community Center
stops.blurb: Flat Creek is barely a dot on the map, seven miles south of Shelbyville — but on the first Saturday of each month it's a hot spot for old-fashioned music and eating.

stops.name: Garland King Museum
stops.blurb: Some museums defy category, and this one-of-a-kind Shelbyville collection is among them. Joe Elmore takes the tour — watch the segment to see what Garland King keeps inside.
